metadata.title: University of Toronto – Policy on Information Technology

controls.title: Purpose and Scope of Information Technologies

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The University of Toronto’s information and communication technology resources provide services and infrastructure to support the University’s academic mission, including learning, teaching, research, administration, and communication.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: This policy applies to all forms of information and communication technology, including hardware, software, network services, and emerging technologies.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Information technologies provided by the University shall be used in a manner consistent with the University’s mission and academic plan.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The use of University information technologies for commercial purposes is prohibited unless written permission is granted by the responsible Vice-President.

groups.title: Introduction and Deployment of New Technologies

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The introduction and deployment of new information technologies must align with the University’s mission, academic plan, and divisional plans.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The design and implementation of new technologies should enhance the curricular, co-curricular, teaching, research, and administrative functions of the University.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The Vice-President and Provost has responsibility for the strategic direction and deployment of institutional information technologies.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The Vice-President and Provost is authorized to establish guidelines and procedures related to the use of information technologies, with changes reported to the appropriate Governing Council body.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Information Technology committees with senior academic and administrative representation shall advise on infrastructure planning and new technology deployment.
